Tests object addition using custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code defines two objects, `object1` and `object2`, each with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods. The `valueOf` method for both objects returns the number `1`, while the `toString` method returns the number `0`. The code then uses an `assert` statement to verify that the sum of `object1` and `object2` equals `2`. This demonstrates how JavaScript uses the `valueOf` method for type conversion when performing arithmetic operations on objects.
